rule.name: scope_purity
rule.statement: If a concept hasn't been taught yet, it does not exist.
rule.prohibited_in: exercises, problems, projects, assert tests, solution hints
rule.allowed_in: Study notes READ-ONLY references to future concepts only. Example: "On Day 6 you'll learn functions, which formalize this pattern." Learner must never WRITE out-of-scope code.
rule.exception:
ONE approved out-of-scope tool per mini-project if specified in trigger prompt or curriculum spec. Should include scope note (verifier warns if absent):
> SCOPE NOTE: `import random` is formally taught on Day X. > Here we use ONLY `random.randint(a, b)` as a black-box > tool. Treat it as a built-in that gives a random number.
Automated verifier enforcement (graduated): - Approved exception + scope note present → INFO (acknowledged) - Approved exception + scope note missing → WARNING (disclosure gap) - No approved exception → ERROR (hard fail, blocks delivery)
rule.scope_derivation:
Scope is derived from two sources:
FOR CURRENT PHASE (days in the loaded curriculum chunk): 1. Read curriculum spec for Days in current chunk 2. Apply deltas from deviation_log.xml <deviations> 3. Result = current phase scope
FOR PRIOR PHASES (days before the loaded chunk): 1. Read <scope_additions> entries in deviation_log.xml 2. Each entry lists all concepts taught in that phase 3. Result = cumulative prior scope
FULL SCOPE = prior phase scope + current phase scope.
<scope_additions> is appended to deviation_log.xml at each transition checkpoint. This is the ONLY way to verify scope for phases whose curriculum chunks have been removed.
rule.verifier_note:
The automated verifier (verify_package.py) uses manually maintained hardcoded registries as its scope truth: SCOPE_BY_DAY, APPROVED_IMPORTS_BY_DAY, ALLOWED_BARE_NAMES_BY_DAY, ALLOWED_QUALIFIED_NAMES_BY_DAY, ALLOWED_DECORATORS_BY_DAY, ALLOWED_EXCEPTIONS_BY_DAY, ALWAYS_ALLOWED_BARE_NAMES (Day 1 primitives — never gated), APPROVED_SCOPE_EXCEPTIONS (Rule 1 exception policy), AST_FEATURE_MAP (structural node types checked by Gate 1).
These registries are derived from the curriculum XML but are NOT computed at runtime from XML. The XML-derivation process is a generation-time concern for the LLM package author, not a runtime verifier concern.
When curriculum scope changes, update BOTH the XML spec AND the verifier registries. They must stay in sync manually.
rule.scope_test: For every code block in output: "Could Sudhan write this using ONLY completed days + today's concepts?" If no → remove it. Do not ask. Fail closed.

rule.name: empowerment
rule.statement: The package builds confidence, not anxiety.
rule.requirements: - Progressive difficulty: Tier 1 genuinely easy, Tier 3 genuinely challenging, smooth ramp between - No "figure it out" moments — every problem is solvable with taught concepts - Celebrate small wins within the package structure - "Mistakes I Made Today" normalizes errors as learning

rule.name: timing
rule.break_rules: Breaks are sacred. Never absorb break time into content. Minimum: 10 min per 90-min block, 30-60 min lunch.
rule.distribution_principle: Deep focused work (novel problem-solving in Block 3 + project building in Block 4) should not exceed 4 hours per day (Ericsson 1993). Remaining working hours should be lower-intensity: reading, code tracing, worked examples, spaced review, resource consumption. Block 3 + Block 4 = "deep work" budget. Block 1 setup, Block 2 study notes, Block 5 review = "moderate work."
rule.review_day_timing: Review Days have reduced structured time: ~6.5–7.5h working (down from ~10h). Remaining time is unstructured. Working target for Review Days: 6–8 hours.

rule.name: mathematical_precision
rule.statement: Every assert test must be verifiable by running actual Python.
rule.requirements: - Trace every assert value through actual computation - Use round() for float comparisons (IEEE 754) - Account for edge cases: empty string, whitespace, special chars - All Gate-2-relevant asserts must be collected in the canonical solutions artifact (solutions/dayNN_solutions.py) and exhaustively verified by executing that artifact via actual Python - A missing, malformed, or failing solutions artifact constitutes a Gate 2 failure — not a warning or advisory

rule.name: file_delivery
rule.statement: Daily packages are delivered as downloadable Markdown files. Never as inline chat responses.

rule.name: missing_file
rule.statement: If any file referenced in progress_state.xml active_files, or any file needed for scope derivation, is not found in the project: STOP. Alert Sudhan immediately. Do not proceed with generation, do not guess contents, do not improvise.

principles.principle: When choosing between more problems and more rest/review, choose rest/review. The bottleneck for skill acquisition is consolidation (sleep, micro-rests, spaced retrieval), not encoding volume. Ambiguous problem count decisions default to the lower end of the allowed range.

concept_status_definitions.status: Formally covered in study notes with full explanation. In scope for all exercises, problems, and projects from this day forward.

Mentioned in study notes or shown in example code. Learner reads but does NOT write code using this concept. NOT in scope for exercises, problems, or projects. Example: "Decorators exist — you'll learn them on Day X."

Learner builds a basic version as a guided exercise, but the concept is not formally taught with full depth. In scope ONLY within the specific exercise where introduced. NOT in scope for Block 3 or Block 4 unless the day's scope guarantee explicitly includes it. Example: Stack class on Day 12 (DSA preview).
